acceptance_targets: [CH07_CRAFT_GROSZ_HANDOUT] In the revised Grosz scene, a cold reader can point to one concrete feature on the annex page that demonstrates skimming or ledger manipulation, one claim Grosz explicitly marks as inference rather than fact, and one immediate obstacle or risk inside the conversation that prevents the meeting from functioning as a full conspiracy briefing. If Grosz still calmly supplies document proof, organizational taxonomy, named leadership clue, and Westalian intelligence sales in one clean explanatory run, the passage fails.

[CH07_DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_REGISTER] After revision, each of the two major conversations contains at least one visibly messy spoken turn such as an interruption, restart, qualification, or unfinished sentence that still reads clearly under scene pressure, and no line is framed by the narrator as having 'landed' or its equivalent. A cold reader should be able to identify whether an unattributed line belongs to Damian, Demetrius, or Grosz based on register alone. If the sharpest lines still read as standalone quotable aphorisms that any of the three men could have said, the revision fails.

[CH07_PROSE_POV_POLISH] In the revised prose at the cited lines, each descriptive beat can be paraphrased as something Damian specifically notices, resents, or feels in real time, not as a free-floating narrator maxim. Across those cited passages, no more than one marked negation/correction flourish remains. If multiple sentences still read like detachable social commentary or caption-ready aphorisms rather than close Damian POV, the revision fails.

[CH07_CRAFT_PHOTO_WITHHOLD] After revision, a cold reader can state exactly why Damian does not complete the identification in this scene, and that reason is grounded in an observable feature of the photo, the memory, or Damian's present knowledge. If the passage still clearly cues Anya while offering no stronger obstacle than abstract 'the name would not come,' the beat fails.
[RED-013] Ch7 does not describe Damian's jaw tightening, locking, or clenching.
[RED-017] Ch7 contains no more than 5 uses of 'something'.
[length_chapter_07_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised Grosz scene, a cold reader can point to one concrete feature on the annex page that demonstrates skimming or ledger manipulation, one claim Grosz explicitly marks as inference rather than fact, and one immediate obstacle or risk inside the conversation that prevents the meeting from functioning as a full conspiracy briefing. If Grosz still calmly supplies document proof, organizational taxonomy, named leadership clue, and Westalian intelligence sales in one clean explanatory run, the passage fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's central discovery arrives as a one-stop briefing from Grosz plus a conveniently preserved file that yields exactly the chapter's needed answers: funding mechanism, coalition structure, suspect name, and bilateral profiteering. That makes the turn feel handed over instead of fought for. The scene keeps telling Damian and the reader what the Renewal Front is in clean thesis language, but it does not create enough uncertainty about what is proved, what is inference, and what Grosz might be wrong about. The result is briefing-itis with convenience-discovery logic at the chapter's main hinge.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the apartment scene around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:193, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:201, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:209, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:221 so the reveal is more evidence-led and less lecture-shaped. Keep Grosz as the source, but make Damian extract meaning from concrete anomalies on the annex page, force Grosz to resist or hedge before giving larger claims, and separate confirmed facts from rumor or old-man pattern recognition. Preserve the coalition/funding/bilateral-rot payload, but do not let one uninterrupted explanation solve all three at once.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, each of the two major conversations contains at least one visibly messy spoken turn such as an interruption, restart, qualification, or unfinished sentence that still reads clearly under scene pressure, and no line is framed by the narrator as having 'landed' or its equivalent. A cold reader should be able to identify whether an unattributed line belongs to Damian, Demetrius, or Grosz based on register alone. If the sharpest lines still read as standalone quotable aphorisms that any of the three men could have said, the revision fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's dominant dialogue register is composed rather than lived. Demetrius and Grosz keep speaking in balanced thesis-lines, aphoristic summaries, mini-speech diagnoses, and mic-drop retorts, while the narration even certifies one line as having 'landed.' That polish also collapses idiolect separation: Damian, Demetrius, and Grosz all sound too close to the same clever, controlled authorial voice. For a pressured private chapter, this is a chapter-level dialogue failure, not an isolated line issue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the lunch exchange and the Grosz conversation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:113, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:213, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:243 so each speaker has friction and a distinct speech logic. Damian should sound more visibly overcontrolled and occasionally self-interrupting when he gets too honest. Demetrius should sound managerial and infrastructural rather than epigrammatic. Grosz should sound partial, older, and wary, with hedges, anecdotal specifics, or stop-start disclosure instead of crisp wisdom lines. Remove narrator admiration of how a line 'landed.'

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised prose at the cited lines, each descriptive beat can be paraphrased as something Damian specifically notices, resents, or feels in real time, not as a free-floating narrator maxim. Across those cited passages, no more than one marked negation/correction flourish remains. If multiple sentences still read like detachable social commentary or caption-ready aphorisms rather than close Damian POV, the revision fails.
findings.problem: Damian's close-third narration keeps drifting from his earnest, self-critical interiority into detachable authorial bons mots and repeated negation/correction flourishes. Lines about weather, restaurants, buildings, and stillness often feel written to be admired as sentences rather than noticed by this specific focalizer in this specific moment. The chapter remains readable, but the prose voice is too polished-from-above for the Damian profile in the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reground the focal prose at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:41, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:137, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:167, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:261 in Damian's immediate concern, embarrassment, resentment, or alertness rather than in stand-alone commentary. Keep only the strongest image or two. Replace the rest with more variable sentence shapes and observations that feel like Damian's mind under pressure instead of a narrator polishing social copy.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, a cold reader can state exactly why Damian does not complete the identification in this scene, and that reason is grounded in an observable feature of the photo, the memory, or Damian's present knowledge. If the passage still clearly cues Anya while offering no stronger obstacle than abstract 'the name would not come,' the beat fails.
findings.problem: The final photo beat over-specifies the classmate connection, then withholds Damian's recognition through generic forgetfulness. The girl is in his Eden class photo, has bright pink hair, stands beside Becky, and is tied to the right school year, yet the passage can only say that the name 'would not come.' Because the scene is plainly planting Anya, the non-recognition reads less like believable psychology and more like author-managed delay.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the photo sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:281, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:285, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:287 so Damian's failure to complete the identification has a concrete on-page reason. Either give the photo or memory a specific limiting condition that blocks recognition, or let him remember Anya's name but stop short of connecting her to the cultural attaché for a rendered reason tied to what he does and does not know yet.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch7 does not describe Damian's jaw tightening, locking, or clenching.
findings.evidence: Ch4 L939: 'His jaw tightened.'; Ch5 L1112: 'His jaw tightened.'; Ch7 L1890: 'Damian's jaw tightened.'
findings.problem: Third use of Damian's jaw-tightening gesture in 4 chapters, same dramatic function (suppressed reaction). The gesture has become Damian's sole physical tell for emotional containment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace with a different physical register. Damian is a diplomat trained in composure — he has a repertoire of tells (voice, breathing, stillness, hand position) beyond jaw tension.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch7 contains no more than 5 uses of 'something'.
findings.evidence: Ch7 'something': 11 occurrences / 4,094 prose words = 1 per 372 words (threshold: 1 per 800). Manuscript-wide 'something': 73 / 56,758 = 1 per 777 words.
findings.problem: Ch7 has the second-highest 'something' density (11 instances, 1 per 372 words). This is Damian's POV chapter; the vague 'something' repeatedly substitutes for precise internal observation in a character whose political training should produce sharper self-assessment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace 'something' with specific nouns or cut the hedging construction. Damian's internal voice should name what he perceives rather than gesturing vaguely at it. Target cutting 6-7 of the 11 instances.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
